hero.name: Leandro Furtado
hero.tagline: I build fullstack apps that ship to production — robust REST APIs with Node.js and .NET, componentized React UIs, and Flutter mobile.

about.label: About me
about.title: Fullstack engineering from MVP to production
about.p1: I'm Leandro Furtado, a fullstack developer with 3+ years of experience. I work on the backend with Node.js and .NET building robust REST APIs, and on the frontend with React crafting componentized, well-integrated UIs.
about.p2: Hands-on with containerized environments (Docker), third-party API integrations and event-driven architectures — I shipped real-time payment confirmation via SignalR/WebSocket in a financial context. Currently a Founding Engineer (mobile) at PayPass, a fintech in production.

items.description: Leading the mobile layer of a fintech in production, from zero to deploy. Building .NET REST APIs with JWT, Pix payment flows with real-time confirmation via SignalR, FCM push and Open Finance integration (Pluggy). Stack: Flutter, .NET, Node.js, Supabase, Docker.
Developing and maintaining an educational system used by municipalities. React frontend with advanced componentization and React Hook Form; .NET backend with Dapper, Entity Framework and PostgreSQL.
Maintenance and evolution of features in the educational system, focused on UX, performance and componentization.
Payment systems with Next.js and TypeScript, focused on usability and responsiveness. REST API integration and collaboration with the backend team.
React development with componentization, Redux state management and API integration.

paypass.name: PayPass
paypass.descriptionShort: Production fintech: Flutter mobile app with advances, Pix, credit score and Open Finance — .NET backend, PostgreSQL data and Firebase services.
paypass.descriptionLong:
PayPass is a production fintech focused on receivables advances and personal finance. I work as Founding Engineer on the mobile layer, from greenfield to app store release.

The Flutter app integrates Pix flows with real-time confirmation (SignalR), push via Firebase Cloud Messaging, JWT auth against .NET APIs and Open Finance integration (Pluggy). Critical persistence and reporting run on PostgreSQL.

The product has a public marketing site; source code and internal details stay confidential — what you see here is only what is appropriate to share on a portfolio.

avaEducacao.name: Studra
avaEducacao.tagline: Virtual learning platform for higher education institutions
avaEducacao.descriptionShort: Multi-tenant LMS (CREATOR + institution): programs, classes, content, grading, forums, polls and calendar — Vite + shadcn on the client; REST API with Node.js, Fastify, Prisma, Zod and Supabase (Auth, Postgres, Storage).
avaEducacao.descriptionLong:
Studra is a learning environment built for everyday academic work: course and class management, tools for instructors, student progress tracking, and communication channels such as forums, polls and an integrated calendar.

On the frontend I used React with Vite and TypeScript, UI with shadcn/ui and Supabase Auth integration. On the backend, a REST API in Node.js with Fastify: Prisma as ORM on PostgreSQL hosted on Supabase, Zod for request and env validation, JWT auth via Supabase, file storage on Supabase Storage, and interactive docs via Scalar/OpenAPI (`GET /docs`). The domain spans two tenant types — CREATOR (content producer: programs → disciplines → cohorts → enrollments) and INSTITUTION (semesters → classes → class disciplines → enrollments) — sharing content modules, assessment (stages, activities, exams, gradebook) and communication.

There is a reference deployment (frontend on Vercel, API on Render) and a public demo login; the product remains classified as company-confidential. Link to the login screen: ava-educacao.vercel.app.

r3Educacao.name: R3+ TopTech · Education system
r3Educacao.descriptionShort: Municipal education platform: modules for the secretariat, school units, teaching staff, admin, pre-enrollment, staff selection and more — React, React Hook Form, .NET and PostgreSQL.
r3Educacao.descriptionLong:
Web platform built at R3+ TopTech for municipal education management. The dashboard brings together many modules — e.g. SEMED settings, school units, teaching (planning and class diary), admin (assets, transport, food), pre-enrollment, staff selection, library, facial recognition and financial transfers.

On the frontend I worked with highly componentized React and React Hook Form. On the backend, APIs and services in .NET with Dapper, Entity Framework and PostgreSQL.

Confidential company project: no public repo or demo; the mockup reflects the UI density and scope of the system.

petshop.name: PetShop System
petshop.tagline: Full petshop management in one place
petshop.descriptionShort: SaaS platform for petshops: smart scheduling with a visual kanban, owner & pet records, and financial control with reports.
petshop.descriptionLong:
PetShop System is a SaaS platform designed for daily petshop operations. It unifies scheduling, owner and pet records, service flow and financial control into a single dashboard.

Planned features include: visual kanban for appointments, Google login, multi-tenant auth, revenue dashboards, recurring grooming bookings and automated owner notifications.

The goal is a clean, fast and responsive experience — UI built with shadcn/ui on Next.js, persistence via Supabase + Prisma, and end-to-end typing with TypeScript.
